texto: Your highest patriotism today is to respect the memory of those who have died in the uniform of their country by vowing that it will never happen again. The basest treason is to permit yourself to shamefully and cowardly follow the false patriots into another war, one surely bringing in its wake even greater disasters for our beloved America than any before.

The basest treason is to permit yourself to shamefully and cowardly follow the false patriots into another war, one surely bringing in its wake even greater disasters for our beloved America than any before.

texto: How the press, for example, loves to brag to its victims— its readers—about its freedom. Yes, the press may be free to lie and distort and suppress and deceive and malign, but is it free to tell the truth?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Willis Carto que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “How the press, for example, loves to brag to its victims— its readers—about its freedom. Yes, the…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, freedom-of-speech, journalism, com foco em estudos e journalism.
